vocab.meaning: lọc bỏ (âm thanh không mong muốn)
vocab.example: Living near a busy intersection, I’ve had to learn how to filter out the constant hum of traffic to get any work done.

vocab.meaning: phát ra tiếng ôm sùm, chói tai (thường là loa đài)
vocab.example: Early in the morning, the communal loudspeakers often blare out news and announcements across the neighborhood.

vocab.meaning: giảm dần rồi tắt hẳn (âm thanh, tiếng ồn)
vocab.example: The street noise usually starts to die down after midnight, finally giving me some much-needed tranquility.

vocab.meaning: trở nên yên lặng hơn (thường dùng để yêu cầu ai đó)
vocab.example: I sometimes wish my neighbors would pipe down when they are having those rowdy late-night parties.

vocab.meaning: trôi vào, vọng vào (âm thanh từ xa)
vocab.example: During the weekends, the faint sound of music from a nearby cafe often drifts in through my window.
In the narrow alleyways of the city, the sound of footsteps tends to echo back, creating a rather eerie atmosphere.

vocab.meaning: ngăn chặn, không cho âm thanh lọt vào
vocab.example: I invested in a pair of high-quality headphones to block out the racket of the construction site next door.

vocab.meaning: đột ngột phát ra tiếng (thường là tiếng nói hoặc âm thanh cao)
vocab.example: Just as the room goes quiet, the car alarms outside often pipe up, which is incredibly startling.

vocab.meaning: làm giật mình khỏi (một trạng thái nào đó)
vocab.example: The sudden screech of brakes in the street can startle me out of a deep sleep.

vocab.meaning: làm giảm bớt độ sắc/váng của âm thanh
vocab.example: The thick curtains in my bedroom help to dull down the sharp noises of the city outside.
